seoTitle: IXYS Solutions | Power Electronics & Motor Drive Solutions | LiTong
seoDescription: IXYS power electronics solutions including motor drives, SMPS, and industrial power systems. Technical support and reference designs available from authorized distributor.

faqs.question: How do I choose the right IXYS solution for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right IXYS solution depends on your power electronics requirements: (1) For motor drives - choose IGBT modules or high-voltage MOSFETs with adequate current and voltage ratings; (2) For switch-mode power supplies - select fast-switching MOSFETs with low gate charge and fast recovery diodes; (3) For DC-DC converters - use low-voltage trench MOSFETs with ultra-low Rds(on) for synchronous rectification; (4) For phase control applications - choose thyristors or triacs with appropriate voltage and current ratings. Consider factors like switching frequency, thermal requirements, efficiency targets, and cost constraints. Contact LiTong FAE for personalized recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match solution to application requirements. Contact FAE for guidance.

faqs.question: What support does LiTong provide for IXYS solutions?
faqs.answer: LiTong provides comprehensive support for IXYS solutions: (1) Technical consultation - helping select the right power semiconductors for your application; (2) Application engineering - supporting power supply design, thermal management, and gate drive design; (3) SPICE models - providing device models for circuit simulation; (4) Reference designs - sharing proven circuit topologies and design examples; (5) Failure analysis - investigating any quality issues with IXYS engineering support; (6) Long-term supply - ensuring stable supply for production programs. Our FAE team has deep expertise in power electronics and IXYS product families.
faqs.decisionGuide: LiTong provides end-to-end support from design to production for IXYS solutions.

faqs.question: Can IXYS provide custom power semiconductor solutions?
faqs.answer: Yes, IXYS offers custom power semiconductor solutions for specific application requirements: (1) Custom voltage and current ratings - devices optimized for specific operating conditions; (2) Special packages - unique form factors for space-constrained designs; (3) Enhanced screening - additional testing for high-reliability applications; (4) Long-term supply agreements - guaranteed availability for extended product lifecycles; (5) Automotive qualification - AEC-Q101 certification for vehicle applications. Custom solutions require minimum order quantities and development lead times. Contact LiTong to discuss your specific requirements and feasibility.
faqs.decisionGuide: Custom solutions available for volume applications. Contact LiTong to discuss requirements.

faqs.question: What is the typical lead time for IXYS solutions?
faqs.answer: IXYS solution lead times vary by complexity: (1) Standard catalog products - typically 8-12 weeks for common configurations; (2) High-volume orders - 10-14 weeks depending on capacity; (3) Custom solutions - 14-20 weeks for non-standard configurations; (4) Samples - 2-4 weeks for engineering samples. LiTong maintains strategic inventory for high-demand IXYS products. For production programs, we recommend forecasting and buffer stock arrangements to ensure continuous supply.
faqs.decisionGuide: Standard solutions 8-12 weeks, custom solutions 14-20 weeks. Contact sales for specific lead times.

faqs.question: Does IXYS offer evaluation platforms for solution development?
faqs.answer: Yes, IXYS offers evaluation resources for solution development: (1) Reference designs - complete schematics and PCB layouts for motor drives, SMPS, and inverters; (2) Evaluation boards - hardware platforms for testing device performance; (3) SPICE models - accurate device models for circuit simulation; (4) Application notes - detailed design guides for various topologies; (5) Thermal models - simulation data for thermal design. Evaluation resources are available for qualified customers. Contact LiTong sales to request development resources for your project.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluation resources available for qualified projects. Contact LiTong sales to request development resources.

solutions.name: Motor Drive Solution
solutions.title: IXYS Motor Drive Solution
solutions.subtitle: High-performance power semiconductor solution for industrial motor drives and servo controls
solutions.description: Complete motor drive solution featuring IXYS IGBT modules and high-voltage MOSFETs optimized for industrial motor control applications.
solutions.longDescription: IXYS Motor Drive Solution provides a comprehensive power semiconductor platform for industrial motor drives and servo control systems. The solution features IXYS XPT IGBT modules and high-voltage MOSFETs with robust avalanche capability, delivering reliable performance in demanding motor control applications. It enables motor drive designs from 1kW to 100kW+ with excellent efficiency and thermal performance. The solution is ideal for variable frequency drives, servo systems, CNC machines, and industrial automation. LiTong provides complete design support including reference designs, gate drive recommendations, and thermal analysis.
benefits: High efficiency with low switching losses

faqs.question: What is the difference between IGBT and MOSFET for motor drives?
faqs.answer: IGBTs and MOSFETs have different optimal operating ranges for motor drives: (1) IGBTs - best for applications above 300V and 20A, lower conduction losses at high currents, typical switching frequency 2-15kHz; (2) MOSFETs - best for applications below 300V or requiring high switching frequencies (>20kHz), lower switching losses, simpler gate drive. For 230V single-phase drives up to 3kW, MOSFETs are often preferred. For 480V three-phase drives above 5kW, IGBTs are typically more efficient. IXYS offers both technologies to optimize for your specific application.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose IGBTs for high-voltage high-power; MOSFETs for low-voltage or high-frequency applications.

faqs.question: What switching frequency is recommended for motor drives?
faqs.answer: Motor drive switching frequency selection involves trade-offs: (1) 2-4kHz - lowest switching losses, audible noise may be issue, typical for high-power drives >50kW; (2) 4-8kHz - good balance of losses and noise, most common for industrial drives 5-50kW; (3) 8-16kHz - reduces audible noise and motor current ripple, higher switching losses, typical for servo drives; (4) 16-20kHz - above audible range, highest switching losses, for sensitive applications. Higher frequencies require better thermal management. IXYS devices are characterized across this frequency range.
faqs.decisionGuide: 4-8kHz is typical for general industrial drives; higher frequencies for servo or noise-sensitive applications.

faqs.question: How do I protect motor drive power semiconductors?
faqs.answer: Motor drive protection strategies: (1) Overcurrent protection - use desaturation detection for IGBTs, current sensing for MOSFETs; (2) Short circuit protection - fast detection (<10µs) and soft shutdown to prevent damage; (3) Overtemperature protection - monitor heatsink or device temperature; (4) Overvoltage protection - use snubbers or active clamping for voltage spikes; (5) Gate drive protection - undervoltage lockout and negative gate voltage. IXYS devices feature robust avalanche capability, providing additional protection against voltage transients. Implement multiple protection levels for reliable operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement comprehensive protection including overcurrent, short circuit, and thermal protection.

faqs.question: What thermal management is required for motor drive IGBTs?
faqs.answer: Motor drive IGBT thermal management: (1) Heatsink sizing - calculate based on total losses (conduction + switching) and maximum junction temperature; (2) Thermal interface - use high-quality thermal pads or grease with <0.5°C·cm²/W thermal resistance; (3) Airflow - forced air cooling typically required for drives >5kW; (4) Thermal simulation - use software to verify temperatures under worst-case conditions; (5) Monitoring - implement temperature sensors for protection and fan control. Example: 15kW drive with 200W losses requires heatsink with Rth < 0.5°C/W with forced air. IXYS provides thermal models for simulation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Size heatsink for worst-case losses with adequate margin; use forced air for drives above 5kW.

faqs.question: Can IXYS devices handle regenerative braking?
faqs.answer: Yes, IXYS power semiconductors handle regenerative braking in motor drives: (1) IGBT modules - include fast recovery freewheeling diodes for brake chopper circuits; (2) MOSFETs - body diodes can conduct brake current, or add external diodes for higher currents; (3) Brake chopper - use IGBT or MOSFET to dissipate regenerative energy in resistor; (4) Active front end - use IGBT modules for regenerative drives feeding energy back to line. For high-inertia loads or frequent braking, size devices for brake current (often 150-200% of motor current). IXYS devices feature robust reverse bias safe operating area (RBSOA) for reliable braking operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: IXYS devices support regenerative braking; size for brake current requirements.

solutions.name: SMPS Solution
solutions.title: IXYS SMPS Solution
solutions.subtitle: High-efficiency power semiconductor solution for switch-mode power supplies
solutions.description: Complete SMPS solution featuring IXYS fast-switching MOSFETs and fast recovery diodes for high-efficiency power supplies.
solutions.longDescription: IXYS SMPS Solution delivers high-efficiency power semiconductors for switch-mode power supply applications. The solution features fast-switching MOSFETs with low gate charge and fast recovery diodes with excellent reverse recovery characteristics. It enables SMPS designs from 100W to 10kW+ with high efficiency and reliability. The solution is ideal for server power supplies, telecom rectifiers, LED drivers, and industrial power supplies. LiTong provides comprehensive design support including topology selection, magnetic design guidance, and EMI mitigation strategies.
benefits: Low EMI with soft recovery diodes

faqs.question: What SMPS topology is best for my application?
faqs.answer: SMPS topology selection depends on power level and requirements: (1) Flyback - best for <150W, simple, low cost, good for multiple outputs; (2) Forward - good for 100-500W, better transformer utilization than flyback; (3) Half-bridge/Full-bridge - for 500W-5kW, efficient, good for high power; (4) LLC resonant - for 100W-1kW, very high efficiency, excellent for server PSUs; (5) Buck/Boost - for DC-DC conversion, simple, efficient. Consider input voltage range, output requirements, efficiency targets, and cost constraints. Contact LiTong FAE for topology recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Flyback for <150W, LLC for high-efficiency 100W-1kW, bridge topologies for >500W.

faqs.question: How do I minimize EMI in SMPS designs?
faqs.answer: EMI minimization techniques for SMPS: (1) Soft switching - use resonant topologies or snubbers to reduce dV/dt and dI/dt; (2) Gate drive - optimize gate resistance to control switching speed; (3) Layout - minimize loop areas in high di/dt paths, use ground planes; (4) Filtering - use proper input and output filters with X and Y capacitors; (5) Shielding - use shielded inductors and transformers; (6) Soft recovery diodes - IXYS fast recovery diodes feature soft recovery to reduce EMI. Start with good layout practices - this is the most cost-effective EMI mitigation strategy.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use soft switching, optimize layout, and select soft recovery diodes to minimize EMI.

faqs.question: What is synchronous rectification and when should I use it?
faqs.answer: Synchronous rectification (SR) replaces diodes with MOSFETs for output rectification: (1) Benefits - reduces rectification losses from 0.5-1V diode drop to I²×Rds(on), improves efficiency 2-5%; (2) Applications - recommended for outputs <12V or currents >10A; (3) Control - requires SR controller IC to drive MOSFET gates with proper timing; (4) Challenges - adds complexity, requires careful timing to avoid shoot-through. For 5V/20A output, SR can save 10-15W compared to diode rectification. IXYS offers low-Rds(on) MOSFETs ideal for SR applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SR for low-voltage high-current outputs (<12V, >10A) to improve efficiency.

faqs.question: How do I select the right MOSFET for my SMPS?
faqs.answer: SMPS MOSFET selection criteria: (1) Voltage rating - 1.5-2x maximum drain voltage including spikes; (2) Current rating - based on thermal constraints, not just maximum current; (3) Rds(on) - lower is better for conduction losses, but consider cost; (4) Gate charge (Qg) - lower Qg reduces gate drive losses and enables faster switching; (5) Output capacitance (Coss) - affects switching losses in hard-switched topologies; (6) Body diode - check reverse recovery for bridge topologies. For high-frequency designs (>200kHz), prioritize low Qg. For high-current designs, prioritize low Rds(on). IXYS provides devices optimized for various SMPS applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Balance Rds(on) and Qg based on operating frequency and current requirements.

faqs.question: What magnetic components are needed for SMPS?
faqs.answer: SMPS magnetic component selection: (1) Transformers - for isolated topologies (flyback, forward, LLC), design for operating frequency, power level, and voltage ratios; (2) Inductors - for buck, boost, and output filters, calculate inductance based on ripple current requirements; (3) Core material - ferrite for high frequency (>50kHz), powdered iron for lower cost applications; (4) Winding - use litz wire for high frequency to reduce skin effect; (5) Gap - gapped cores prevent saturation in inductors and flyback transformers. Work with magnetic component suppliers or use design software. LiTong FAE can provide guidance on magnetic design for IXYS-based SMPS.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design magnetics for operating frequency and power level; use ferrite cores for high frequency.
